chapter_title.en: Chapter on Whose Testimony Must Be Rejected and Whose Testimony Must Be Accepted

text: 3310 - وَ سَأَلَ عُبَيْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ اَلْحَلَبِيُّ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ شَهَادَةِ اَلْقَابِلَةِ فِي اَلْوِلاَدَةِ قَالَ "تَجُوزُ شَهَادَةُ اَلْوَاحِدَةِ وَ شَهَادَةُ اَلنِّسَاءِ فِي اَلْمَنْفُوسِ وَ اَلْعُذْرَةِ ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3310 - And Ubaydullah bin Ali Al-Halabi asked Abu Abdullah (as), about the testimony of a midwife in childbirth.
Imam (as) said: "The testimony of one woman is accepted, and so is the testimony of women regarding the newborn and the virginity (of a girl)."
